title: Analyzing Wells Fargo Bank Statements: A Lender's Guide
meta: Wells Fargo bank statements have a unique format. Learn how to navigate and analyze Wells Fargo statements for income verification, MCA detection, and risk assessment.
content:
Wells Fargo Statement Format Overview

Wells Fargo is one of the top four banks in the United States, making their bank statements a common document in mortgage and business loan underwriting. Their statement format differs slightly from Chase and Bank of America, with some unique characteristics to be aware of.

Wells Fargo Statement Sections

  • Account Activity Summary: Beginning balance, deposits/credits, withdrawals/debits, and ending balance
  • Deposits and Other Credits: All incoming transactions listed chronologically with date, description, and amount
  • Withdrawals and Other Debits: All outgoing transactions in reverse chronological order
  • Service Charges and Fees: Monthly fees, overdraft fees, and other charges

Wells Fargo Direct Deposit Labels

Direct deposits in Wells Fargo statements typically appear with descriptions containing 'DIR DEP' or the employer's payroll provider name (ADP, Paychex, Gusto, etc.). Business ACH payments may show just the company name or a truncated version.

Wells Fargo ATM and Debit Transactions

Wells Fargo includes all debit card and ATM transactions in the withdrawals section. Large or frequent ATM withdrawals can indicate cash-based expenses not visible in the statement — sometimes relevant for income or expense analysis.

Wells Fargo Overdraft and Overdraft Protection

Wells Fargo's overdraft protection program can mask some financial stress — the bank covers overdrafts using a linked account or line of credit. When reviewing Wells Fargo statements, check for 'OVERDRAFT PROTECTION TRANSFER' entries which indicate the primary account was overdrawn even if no NSF fee was charged.

Automated Wells Fargo Statement Analysis

StatementScrub handles Wells Fargo statements natively, processing all sections and formats to extract complete financial data for lending analysis.
